title: user — field classification
description: User carries administrative metadata (tenant + application + status). Profile is the PII cluster — every column is regulated at least at the `confidential` tier, some at `restricted_minor` when the underlying Identity is a minor. TenantMember is administrative. The redactor pipeline (from telemetry) is applied on egress to any Profile field unless the caller carries the `profile.view.pii` permission.

restricted: Profile.first_name (when subject is a minor per identity->dob age-gate)
Profile.dob (always — exposes an age computation; access requires `profile.view.pii` OR the caller IS the subject)
regulated_minor: Profile.avatar_url (minor subject — avatar can be a photo of a child)
audit_log: User.deleted_at (soft-delete + hard-delete written to `audits` + `audit_events` retained 7 years)
Profile.dob (mutations — immutable in practice, but audit any edit)
TenantMember.role_id (every role change is a compliance signal)

notes: The redactor pipeline (from telemetry::RedactorProcessor) applies to every Profile field on egress unless the caller's permission set contains `profile.view.pii`. Minors are gated harder: the redactor never returns the field, even to caller with `profile.view.pii`, unless they ALSO hold `profile.view.pii.child` and the subject is linked as their ward via `sports::AthleteGuardian`.
User.identity_id + Profile.dob together are enough to reconstruct age, so both are `internal` at minimum. Do not expose either outside the auth-service boundary.
TenantMember.metadata is an opaque JSON dictionary used by extending modules (subscription seat notes, HR notes) and treated as `internal` even when the parent User is public.

GDPR Art. 17 (right to erasure): soft-deleted User rows keep their Profile row for the hold period (`user.privacy.erasure_hold_days`, default 30) so the tenant admin can restore. After the hold, `PurgeErasedUsersJob` hard-deletes both.
